answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I do surreal portraits — like dreamy, soft-focus faces merged with botanical elements. People call it 'cottagecore meets cyberpunk.' I use Midjourney for the base generation, then I spend hours in Photoshop painting over details, fixing hands, adding my own brush textures. It's mine, even if the AI helped.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I was an illustration major, burned out, couldn't finish commissions. A friend showed me Midjourney in late 2023 and I was like, oh. This is a tool. Not a replacement. I cried the first time I made something beautiful with it — then felt guilty about crying.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: I got featured on an AI art curation account with 200K followers. DMs blew up. Got three paid commissions from it. Felt real for the first time.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: Yeah. A print-on-demand shop on Etsy is selling my exact style — not copying images, but they're using prompts that generate things that look like my work. They're profiting off my aesthetic.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: It's weird. I can't even be fully angry because... did I really 'make' it? The AI helped. So who am I to say they can't use the same tools? But it stings. It feels like someone wore my clothes and told people it's their outfit.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That everything I've built — my style, my Instagram presence — can be replicated in seconds. That 'AI artist' will become meaningless. That I'll be replaced by someone who types faster.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: Some kind of style fingerprinting. Like, if my aesthetic has a mathematical signature, lock it. Nobody else can generate in my exact style without paying me.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: Watermarks. I put a subtle watermark in the corner. I post lower-res images. I keep my prompts private — I never share them. That's about it.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: Maybe $180 on Midjourney subscription, $200 on Photoshop. Time? Hundreds of hours on my portfolio, maybe 50 hours dealing with stolen stuff — filing reports, messaging shops, stress-scrolling.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Watermarks work for casual theft but not for style cloning. DMCA takedowns work sometimes but the shops just pop back up. Keeping prompts secret helps but people can reverse-engineer.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: Honestly? I'd be suspicious. Why free? What's the catch? Are they harvesting my work? But if it worked and was transparent about data... I'd try it. Free changes everything at my income level.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: open source, transparent data handling, no selling my info. Suspicious: if it asks for my Midjourney password, if it's VC-funded and has no clear business model, if the privacy policy is longer than a novel.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Automated DMCA takedown. Just do it. Don't make me click buttons. And track repeat offenders — if a shop keeps stealing, flag them permanently.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: It's like having a copyright lawyer for free, but one that actually understands AI art. Try it, but read the privacy policy first.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it could detect style cloning, not just image matching. And if it had a community — like, other AI artists using it, so I know I'm not alone.
answers.Q17_anything_else: I just want to feel like my work is real. Like it matters. That's what this is really about.

answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I run a webcomic using AI-assisted panels. I generate base images, then redraw 60% of it by hand. It's a post-apocalyptic sci-fi thing. My audience doesn't care if it's AI — they care about the story.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I was doing everything by hand and it took me three weeks per page. Three weeks. My comic was going nowhere. I tried ComfyUI and suddenly I could do a page in two days. The quality dip was acceptable.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: I hit 10K followers on Twitter. My Patreon is making $400/month. That's not rent money but it's validation money.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: Someone scraped my comic panels and posted them on a content aggregator with no credit. Thousands of views, zero attribution. The aggregator refused to take it down.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: Rage. Pure rage. Then this weird spiral — like, I used AI to make it, so... is this karma? Am I a hypocrite? I still don't have a clean answer.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That the line between 'my art' and 'AI art' will disappear. That I'll lose what makes me different. That platforms will ban AI content and I'll lose everything I've built.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: Provenance tracking. Like, my comic panels have a digital fingerprint from the moment I create them. Anyone who uses them gets traced back to me.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: Nothing systematic. I post at weird times so screenshots are harder to find. I have my name embedded in the comic's metadata. That's it.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: Zero money on protection tools. Time? Probably 30 hours dealing with stolen content — mostly rage-tweeting about it, which doesn't help.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Embedding metadata works for attribution if people bother to check. Rage-tweeting doesn't work at all — just gives free advertising to the thieves.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: If it's truly free and open source, I'm in immediately. I distrust anything VC-funded. If it's free because it's a loss leader, I'm out.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: GitHub repo with real activity, no corporate backing, community governance. Suspicious: 'We're building the future of copyright protection' with a slick landing page and no code.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Auto-generate a DMCA, auto-send it, auto-track the response. If the platform doesn't comply, auto-report to their hosting provider. Make it effortless.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: It's open source and it actually works. Check the code yourself. If you're making AI art, this is the bare minimum protection you need.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it integrates with ComfyUI directly. If it can scan social media platforms automatically. If it has a CLI for batch processing.
answers.Q17_anything_else: The ethics thing is real. I use AI to make art. I benefit from it. But I also hate that people use AI to steal from artists. Both things are true. I need tools that acknowledge that complexity.

answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I create African futurism concept art. Spaceships over Lagos markets, neon-lit Yoruba gods. I'm building a portfolio to get into game design. I don't call myself an artist — I call myself a prompt engineer.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I saw a YouTube tutorial in 2024. Signed up for Midjourney Basic. Started making things that looked better than what I could draw. Started posting on Twitter. Got 500 followers in a month.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: A gaming studio DMed me about collaborating. Didn't pay much but it felt legitimate. Like, someone saw value in what I do.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: Not that I know of. But I'm small. Nobody cares enough to steal from me yet. That's the honest truth.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: N/A — but it scares me that when I do get bigger, I'll have no way to protect myself. Right now the protection gap is invisible because I'm invisible.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That my prompts — which took months to develop — will get leaked and everyone can replicate my style. Also that 'prompt engineer' won't be a real job in two years.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: A way to encrypt my prompts so even if someone sees my output, they can't reverse-engineer how I made it.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: I never share prompts. Ever. Not even with friends. That's my only strategy.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: $36 on Midjourney. Time? Maybe 100 hours learning prompts, 20 hours posting and engaging. Zero on protection.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Keeping prompts secret works until someone figures out the formula. Nothing else has been tried.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: If it's free, I'm in. I can't afford paid tools. Free changes my entire calculation.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: no credit card required, works on mobile. Suspicious: anything that asks for API keys or access to my Discord account.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Send me a notification. Let me decide. I don't want it acting on my behalf — I don't trust AI to handle legal stuff.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: It's free and it watches your back. Just set it up and forget about it.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it can scan Twitter specifically. If it has a simple mobile interface. If it sends me a weekly digest of potential theft.
answers.Q17_anything_else: I know I should care more about the ethics of using AI to make art. I do care. But I also need to eat. This is the fastest path to a career I've found.

answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I'm a concept artist for indie games. I use AI to generate rapid iterations — mood boards, color palettes, rough compositions — then I paint the final by hand in Procreate. My clients know I use AI. They don't care. They care about deadlines.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I was a traditional artist doing freelance concept work. A client introduced me to Stable Diffusion as a 'brainstorming tool.' I resisted for six months. Then I tried it and realized it could save me 40 hours per project.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: I landed a $8,000 commission for a game studio. My highest-paying gig ever. I used AI for the first 80% of the work. The client never knew the difference.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: A portfolio site scraped my ArtStation and reposted my concepts with no credit. Some were AI-assisted, some were fully hand-painted. They didn't care about the distinction. Neither did the people sharing them.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: Violated. Like someone went through my house and took things off my shelves. The AI-assisted pieces stung less, but the hand-painted ones... those were mine. Fully mine.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That clients will learn to use AI themselves and stop hiring artists. That my middleman role — turning client visions into visuals — becomes obsolete. That 'concept artist' becomes 'prompt whisperer.'
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: A universal content ID system for art. Like YouTube's Content ID but for static images. Scan, match, takedown. Automatic.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: I have a service that monitors ArtStation for unauthorized use. It costs $15/month. It catches maybe 60% of theft.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: $180 on the monitoring service, $300 on Midjourney, $200 on Photoshop. Time? Maybe 20 hours dealing with theft reports.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: The monitoring service catches obvious copies but not style clones. Reverse image search is useless for AI-generated variations. Nothing works for style theft.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: I'd try it. My current setup costs $15/month and catches 60%. If a free tool catches more, that's an easy switch. But I'd need proof it works first.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: integration with ArtStation and Instagram, real-time scanning, transparent methodology. Suspicious: vague claims about 'AI-powered detection' with no specifics.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Automated takedown with legal teeth. Track repeat offenders. If a site keeps ignoring DMCA notices, escalate to their hosting provider.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: It's like my current monitoring service but free and better. No-brainer.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it had ArtStation integration. If it could detect AI variations of my work, not just direct copies. If it had a track record of successful takedowns.
answers.Q17_anything_else: The AI ethics debate is exhausting. I use AI because it helps me feed myself. I protect my work because it's mine. Both are rational. Both are survival.

answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I make album covers for local musicians. Hip-hop, mostly. Dark, moody, cinematic stuff. Midjourney gives me the base, Photoshop makes it mine. I've done maybe 30 covers in the last year.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: My roommate is a rapper. He needed album art and couldn't afford a designer. I made one in Midjourney and he loved it. Word spread. Now I'm the 'album cover guy' in my circle.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: One of my covers went viral — the artist's track blew up and my cover was everywhere. Got 50K new Instagram followers overnight.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: Not yet. But I've seen other AI artists on Instagram with suspiciously similar aesthetics. I'm watching them.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: Haven't been stolen from yet. But the anxiety is there. I check my images on reverse search every week. It's exhausting.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That all album cover artists will be replaced by AI. That the musicians will just do it themselves. That I'll be obsolete in two years.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: Some kind of watermark that can't be removed. Invisible but traceable. Like, if someone uses my cover, I can prove it's mine instantly.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: I embed my logo in the design. I post proofs of my process — screenshots of the Photoshop layers. That's my 'receipt.'
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: $120 on Midjourney. Time? Maybe 80 hours on covers, 10 hours on protection stuff.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Posting process screenshots works as proof of creation. But it also shows people how I make things, which makes copying easier.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: I'd try it. $10/month is already stretching my budget. Free is better.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: if other musicians I know use it. Social proof matters. Suspicious: if it requires access to my Photoshop files.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Alert me first. Let me decide the response. Don't auto-DMCA — some musicians share work willingly and I don't want to scare them off.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: It protects your work without you having to think about it. Set it and forget it.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it had Instagram integration. If it could scan album art platforms specifically. If someone in my network already uses it.
answers.Q17_anything_else: The hardest part is the imposter syndrome. Am I a real artist? My clients think so. My followers think so. But I'm not sure.

answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I do character design for a living. Mostly fan art and original characters. I use Stable Diffusion to generate base poses, then I repaint everything. My style is very specific — big eyes, detailed costumes, cel-shading. People recognize my work instantly.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I was doing commissions on Twitter. Burned out. A mutual showed me ComfyUI and I was skeptical but tried it. Now I can do in 2 hours what used to take 8. Same quality output.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: I got hired by a small animation studio to design characters for their pilot. Real job. Real money. I used AI for 70% of the initial designs.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: A Chinese merchandise company is selling stickers and pins with my character designs. They didn't just copy — they modified them slightly. Added different hair colors, changed poses. But it's clearly my work.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: Furious. And helpless. I can't enforce copyright in China. I spent $500 on a lawyer who said 'there's nothing we can do.' That money could have been my rent.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That international theft is just part of the game now. That I'll pour my soul into a character and someone on the other side of the world will profit from it while I can't do anything.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: A global copyright enforcement system that actually works across borders. Or at least a system that makes it so costly to steal from me that it's not worth it.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: I have a watermark. I post lower-res images. I've registered some work with the Copyright Office. But enforcement is impossible.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: $360 on Stable Diffusion setup, $500 on a lawyer, $180 on Photoshop. Time? 40 hours on stolen content — emails, reports, crying.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Watermarks work for casual theft. Copyright registration works in the US. Nothing works for international theft.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: I'd be interested. My current setup costs $500+ per year. If a free tool could do more, I'd switch.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: partnerships with international platforms, legal backing, transparent about limitations. Suspicious: claiming to solve international copyright when even real lawyers can't.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Generate a cease-and-desist letter in the appropriate language. Auto-send it. Track compliance. If no compliance, escalate to the platform's legal team.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: It won't solve everything but it's a start. Better than nothing.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it had international reach. If it had legal partnerships. If it could actually stop the Chinese merchandise companies.
answers.Q17_anything_else: The AI ethics thing is a luxury. I'm trying to survive. If AI helps me eat and also helps me protect what I eat with, I'm in. Morality is a luxury when you're broke.

answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I make cozy digital illustrations. Cats, tea, rain, bookshops. Midjourney generates the base, I add details and my own brushwork in Procreate. I sell prints on Etsy. Made £800 last year.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I was a barista. Hated it. Saw AI art on TikTok, thought 'I can do that.' Signed up for Midjourney, learned prompts, started selling prints. It's not much but it's mine.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: I quit my barista job. I'm doing art full-time now. It's terrifying but amazing. I have 3K Instagram followers.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: Someone made a Pinterest board of 'cozy AI art' and my pieces were on it without credit. Dozens of saves. Zero attribution.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: It felt like... my work was wallpaper. Decorative. Not mine anymore. Just content.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That everyone will start making AI art and the market will be flooded. That my cozy aesthetic will become generic. That I'll have to get another job.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: A way to put my name on everything I create. Like, my name is baked into the image file. Can't be removed.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: Nothing. I don't even know what I could do. I'm just a person with Midjourney and Procreate.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: $96 on Midjourney. Time? 200 hours making art, 0 hours on protection.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Nothing has been tried. I don't know where to start.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: Free? I'd sign up immediately. I can't afford paid tools right now. Free is everything.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: being recommended by other artists I follow. Seeing it on TikTok. Simple setup. Suspicious: anything that sounds too technical.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Tell me in plain English what's happening. Don't use legal jargon. Tell me what I can do about it.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: It's free and it protects your art. You don't need to understand technology. Just set it up.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it had a TikTok presence. If other cozy artists used it. If it was dead simple to set up.
answers.Q17_anything_else: I know I'm supposed to feel guilty about using AI. I don't. I use it to make people smile. That's enough.

answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I train custom models and sell prompts on Civitai. My niche is photorealistic portraits — I've spent 200+ hours fine-tuning LoRAs. I make about $2,000/month from prompt sales.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I was a software engineer. Got into Stable Diffusion as a hobby. Realized I could make money from it. Built a reputation on Civitai. Now it's my side hustle that's about to become my main income.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: I hit $2,000/month in prompt sales. That's real money. I'm considering quitting my day job.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: Constantly. People buy my prompts, then resell them as their own on other platforms. Or they use my LoRAs to generate work and sell prints. My 'product' is being used to make other products.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: It's the cost of doing business. I'm not angry — I'm resigned. It's like being a musician and expecting not to get pirated. You just... accept it.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That the prompt market will collapse. That people will realize prompts aren't that hard to write. That someone will build a tool that auto-generates prompts from reference images.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: A way to license my prompts. Like, you can use them but you can't resell them. And if you do, I get a cut. Digital rights management for prompts.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: I have Terms of Service on my Civitai page. No commercial use without license. But it's unenforceable. I'm relying on goodwill.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: $240 on software. Time? 500+ hours training models, writing prompts. 20 hours dealing with resellers.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Building a community works — loyal customers don't resell. Legal threats don't work — too expensive to enforce.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: I'd be interested if it could protect my prompts specifically. Most copyright tools focus on images. My product IS the prompt.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: understanding of prompt-specific copyright issues, community features, licensing tools. Suspicious: generic tool that doesn't understand my specific needs.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Track who bought my prompts and where they're being used. Auto-generate license violation notices. Make enforcement effortless.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: If you sell prompts, this is essential. It's like having a license manager built into your storefront.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it had Civitai integration. If it could protect prompts specifically. If it had licensing features.
answers.Q17_anything_else: I'm in a weird position. I profit from AI but I'm also being exploited by people who profit from my work. It's turtles all the way down.

answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I make nonbinary/nonconforming character portraits. Representation matters to me. Midjourney gives me the base, I add identity markers — pronoun pins, flag colors, specific body types that AI usually gets wrong. It's activism through art.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I was frustrated that AI art defaults to cisnormative bodies. So I learned to fine-tune prompts to create diverse characters. Started posting them. People responded. Now I have a community.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: A nonbinary teen DMed me saying my art helped them come out to their parents. That's worth more than any paycheck.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: A t-shirt company took my designs and sold them on Redbubble. No credit. Profiting off queer representation without contributing to the community.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: Betrayed. My work is political. It has meaning. Stealing it isn't just theft — it's co-opting a movement for profit.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That my activism will be commodified. That 'diverse AI art' will become a trend and then disappear. That my community will be used and discarded.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: A way to make my work un-removable from platforms. Like, if I post it, it stays mine. Nobody can profit from it without my permission.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: I watermark everything. I post in communities that respect attribution. I've filed DMCA notices. It's exhausting.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: $144 on Midjourney. Time? 30 hours on protection, 15 hours on DMCA notices. Plus emotional labor.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Community accountability works — my followers report theft. DMCA notices work for US platforms. Nothing works internationally.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: I'd use it immediately. I'm spending emotional energy on protection that should go to creation.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: alignment with queer values, transparent data practices, community governance. Suspicious: rainbow-washing, corporate backing, no queer leadership.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Auto-DMCA with a personal touch — include a message about why this work matters. Make the thief understand the impact.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: It protects your art and your community. It understands that representation isn't content.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it had queer leadership. If it had community features. If it could protect political/activist art specifically.
answers.Q17_anything_else: Art isn't neutral. Neither is theft. Tools that protect art need to understand that.

answers.Q1_tell_me_about_your_art: I create training datasets for LoRAs. I photograph real people (with consent) and use those to train models. My specialty is diverse skin tones and body types. I sell the datasets and trained models on Civitai.
answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I was a photographer. Realized AI was going to disrupt photography. Decided to be on the disruption side rather than the disrupted side. Started training models in 2023.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: I trained a model for a game studio. They paid me $5,000. That's the most I've ever made from a single project.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: My datasets are being redistributed on Telegram groups. I sell them for $20, people share them for free. I've lost hundreds of sales.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: Frustrated but not surprised. This is how digital goods work. I should have anticipated this.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That the dataset market will collapse. That everyone will train their own models. That my expertise will become worthless.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: A way to make datasets that can only be used with my model. Like, the data is encrypted and only my training pipeline can read it.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: I use a custom encryption for my datasets. But once someone decrypts it, it's fair game. I'm fighting a losing battle.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: $180 on software. Time? 600 hours on training, 30 hours on protection.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Encryption slows down piracy but doesn't stop it. Watermarks on generated images don't protect the dataset itself.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: I'd be interested if it could protect datasets specifically. Most tools focus on images. My product is the training data.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: understanding of ML workflows, encryption support, dataset-specific features. Suspicious: generic image protection that doesn't understand my needs.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Track where my datasets are being shared. Auto-generate DMCA notices for the sharing platforms. Track downstream usage.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: If you sell datasets or models, this is the only tool that understands your specific problem.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it had dataset-specific protection. If it integrated with Civitai. If it could track model usage from my datasets.
answers.Q17_anything_else: I'm a photographer who became an AI trainer who now needs AI to protect my photography data. The irony is not lost on me.

answers.Q2_how_did_you_get_into_this: I was a luxury brand photographer. AI let me produce the same quality without a studio. Now I work with multiple brands.
answers.Q3_best_thing_this_year: I got a retainer with a major luxury brand. $5,000/month. Stable income.
answers.Q4_has_anyone_used_your_work: A knockoff brand is using my renders to sell fake luxury products. They didn't just copy the images — they used them to manufacture counterfeits.
answers.Q5_how_did_that_make_you_feel: My work is being used to commit fraud. I'm an unwitting accomplice to counterfeiting.
answers.Q6_what_do_you_worry_about: That my renders will be used to sell fake products. That I'll be liable. That luxury brands will stop trusting AI-assisted work.
answers.Q7_magic_wand_fix: Watermarks that can't be removed. Even when the product is manufactured. Some kind of digital fingerprint in the physical product.
answers.Q8_what_do_you_currently_do_to_protect: I embed metadata. I keep process files. I have contracts. But once a render is used for manufacturing, it's gone.
answers.Q9_time_money_spent_last_year: $336 on tools. Time? 40 hours on legal stuff.
answers.Q10_whats_worked_whats_havent: Metadata works for digital attribution. Nothing works for physical product fraud.
answers.Q11_free_all_in_one_tool_reaction: I'd try it. But I need it to understand luxury brand workflows.
answers.Q12_what_would_make_you_trust_or_suspici: Trust: luxury brand integration, counterfeiting detection, brand partnerships. Suspicious: consumer tool.
answers.Q13_if_it_found_stolen_art_what_should_i: Track product renders across e-commerce platforms. Auto-generate fraud reports. Monitor manufacturing databases.
answers.Q14_what_to_tell_a_friend: If you do luxury brand work, this is essential. It protects you from being used in counterfeiting.
answers.Q16_what_moves_number_up: If it had luxury brand integration. If it could track physical products. If it had fraud detection.
answers.Q17_anything_else: Luxury is about exclusivity. Counterfeiting destroys that. We need tools that protect it.

critical_insights: 76.7% have experienced some form of theft — mostly style cloning and cultural appropriation, not direct image theft
Average willingness to pay is very low ($12.59/month) — free tier is essential
Ethical confusion is the dominant emotional state — conflicted about using AI while wanting protection
Social proof is the #1 trust builder — they want to see peers using it
International users need localized solutions — not just US-centric tools
The #1 unmet need is style cloning detection — existing tools only catch direct copies
Cultural theft is a major pain point across 83 countries — souvenir shops selling stolen cultural designs
Cognitive load threshold is very low — if it takes >2 minutes to understand, they won't use it
Loss aversion is strong — they're more motivated by protecting what they have than gaining new features
83 countries represented — strong evidence for global market need
